channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: What happens on Inauguration Day? A schedule of key events
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump's inauguration coincides with Martin Luther King Jr. Day — a rare occurrence.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: House Republicans face massive debt problem
item.description: House Republicans have a problem. They want to pass a massive agenda for President-elect Trump, preferably in his first 100 days of office. And they don’t want to add to the federal deficit. That looks impossible.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Biden gives Pope Francis highest civilian honor before leaving office
item.description: Biden, who is only the second Catholic president in U.S. history, spoke with the Pope by phone on Saturday to tell him he was a recipient of the medal, which is the highest civilian honor.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Special counsel Jack Smith resigns after 2-year stint at Department of Justice
item.description: Special counsel Jack Smith left his position at the Department of Justice Friday, according to a court document filed Saturday. He was hired by Merrick Garland in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Carolina's new Democratic governor pledges to rebuild after Helene, work with GOP
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — North Carolina Gov. Josh Stein kicked off his term in office Saturday by praising the resilience of the state's residents following Hurricane Helene and pledging to “cut through red tape to help people rebuild quicker.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden honors Pope Francis with the Presidential Medal of Freedom
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Saturday honored Pope Francis with the Presidential Medal of Freedom with distinction, the highest civilian award given by the president, saying the pontiff was “a light of faith, hope, and love that shines brightly across the world.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Congress braces for California wildfire fallout
item.description: House lawmakers are bracing for what could be a contentious battle over emergency spending in the wake of the wildfires that are tearing through Southern California — an unprecedented disaster that’s already estimated to have caused more than $50 billion in damage in and around Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Many Democrats don't think they'll see a woman become president, AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Democrats are harboring strong feelings of stress and gloom as the new year begins. And many are questioning whether their party's commitment to diverse candidates — especially women — may lead to further political struggles in the Donald Trump era.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Schools nationwide brace for Trump, including measures compelling teachers not to cooperate with ICE
item.description: K-12 school districts, universities and state-level education leaders in the U.S. are drawing up plans to protect students from Trump's immigration enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gov. Hochul defends congestion pricing, explains inflation rebate checks
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – In a few days, New York Gov. Kathy Hochul will deliver her State of the State address.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: TikTok gets frosty reception at Supreme Court in fight to stave off ban
item.description: TikTok received a frosty reception in its fight to save the platform at the Supreme Court, which during oral arguments Friday expressed sympathy with the government’s national security concerns about the platform’s ties to China.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: US streamlines deportation flights to China
item.description: The Biden administration repatriated more than 500 Chinese nationals in the last year, laying the groundwork for systematic deportations across the Pacific Ocean.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: President-elect Trump appears virtually in court to be sentenced in hush money case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump is appearing virtually in a New York courtroom to be sentenced for his hush money conviction, 10 days before he is inaugurated.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: President-elect Trump to be sentenced in hush money case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — In a singular moment in U.S. history, President-elect Donald Trump faces sentencing Friday for his New York hush money conviction after the nation's highest court refused to intervene.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: 7 notable moments between VIPs at Carter’s funeral
item.description: Former President Carter’s funeral service at Washington National Cathedral brought together all five living presidents and current and former officials for an event full of notable interactions.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: The Biden administration succeeds in temporarily blocking a plea deal for accused 9/11 mastermind
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration succeeded Thursday in temporarily blocking accused 9/11 mastermind Khalid Sheikh Mohammed from entering a guilty plea in a deal that would spare him the risk of execution for al-Qaida’s Sept. 11, 2001, attacks.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Mayor Eric Adams delivers 4th State of the City address
item.description: HARLEM, Manhattan (PIX11) – New York City Mayor Eric Adams delivered his fourth State of the City address at The Apollo in Harlem on Thursday. Adams discussed topics like public safety, the cost of living, and new initiatives to build more in the city.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Ex-FBI informant who fabricated bribery story about Biden and his son Hunter gets 6 years in prison
item.description: A former FBI informant who fabricated a story about President Joe Biden and his son Hunter accepting bribes that became central to Republicans’ impeachment effort was sentenced Wednesday to six years in prison.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Exxon sues California AG for defamation after he sued them for 'misleading' plastic recycling claims
item.description: A major oil company has responded to California Attorney General Rob Bonta with a lawsuit alleging defamation after he accused them of misleading customers.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, Fox 5

item.title: Trump refuses to rule out use of military force to take control of Greenland, Panama Canal
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday said he would not rule out the use of military force to seize control of the Panama Canal and Greenland, as he declared U.S. control of both to be vital to American national security.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and ZEKE MILLER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jimmy Carter eulogized by Kamala Harris and others as 39th president returns to Washington
item.description: Nearly 44 years after Jimmy Carter left the nation's capital in humbling defeat, the 39th president returned to Washington for three days of state funeral rites starting Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump Jr. plans Greenland visit after father stirs controversy
item.description: Donald Trump Jr. is planning to head to Greenland for a day-long trip just weeks after his father, President-elect Trump, said U.S. ownership of the Danish territory "is an absolute necessity.
item.dc:creator: Sarakshi Rai, Fox 5

item.title: Politicians reflect on January 6, shifting landscape of power transitions
item.description: The 2024 Presidential Election results have been officially certified by members of Congress. While the session typically happens without fanfare, there was extra security in place this time.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hush money judge refuses to delay Trump's criminal sentencing
item.description: The New York judge who oversaw President-elect Trump’s criminal trial last spring declined his request to pause Friday’s sentencing, setting up a last-minute appellate battle as he seeks to stave off the proceeding.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Trump pitches 'merged' US, Canada after Trudeau resignation announcement
item.description: After Trudeau’s announcement Monday, Trump touted the idea that many Canadians would “LOVE” the idea of being the "51st State" of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Pence: 'Admirable' that Harris certified Trump's win after losing election
item.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence, who had to be evacuated four years ago Monday as rioters stormed the Capitol, welcomed "the return of order and civility" as Congress moved to certify President-elect Donald Trump's 2024 victory.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Trump lawyers push to halt Jan. 10 hush money case sentencing
item.description: President-elect Trump’s legal team urged a judge to hold off on sentencing him on Jan. 10 on his New York criminal conviction ahead of his inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5
